title: Apricot Chutney
excerpt: This time of year fresh apricots are plentiful. It's little effort but great pleasure to cook your own chutney. How strong you want to make it is up to you. Mix it with cream cheese for a dip, serve with cheese and pickles, mix with mayo and make a …
html:
This time of year fresh apricots are plentiful. It's little effort but great pleasure to cook your own chutney. How strong you want to make it is up to you. Mix it with cream cheese for a dip, serve with cheese and pickles, mix with mayo and make a pasta salad.

INGREDIENTS

  • 3 kg apricots halved and pitted
  • 5 fresh chillies (can be left out or more added)
  • 6 onions
  • thumb-sized piece of ginger
  • 4 cloves garlic
  • 10 ml salt
  • 10 ml paprika
  • 15 ml cumin
  • 40 ml curry powder
  • 20 ml turmeric
  • 5 ml chilli flakes (can be left out)
  • 1.5 kg sugar (can use less if you don't like it too sweet)
  • 1 litre brown vinegar
  • 500 ml seedless raisins
  • 1 packet dates

METHOD

  1. Chop the apricots, chillies, onions, ginger, garlic and dates roughly in your food processor.
  2. Now put all the ingredients into a pot and cook for 60 minutes until it becomes thick and syrupy.
  3. If you want it even thicker, mix 90 ml maizena with 100 ml water. Stir it in and cook through.
  4. Sterilise your little bottles in boiling water. Fill them with chutney, close them up and store.
  5. I got 12 little bottles out of it.
